### 289 · JAMES PRESCOTT-HARLOW

**Tier:** 3
**Faction:** Survey Corps — Second Generation, Expedition Commander
**Status:** Active — commanding missions with one hand tied behind his back
**Age:** 45

**Wound:** In Year 104 he commanded an expedition to World 496 that discovered a set of structures beneath the surface — smooth, geometric, composed of the same material as the fourteen-meter mineral layer. He documented them thoroughly. Corps command reviewed his findings, classified them, and issued a standing order: no Survey Corps expedition was to excavate below fourteen meters on any frontier world without direct authorization from the Corps Commandant. The authorization process takes months. In effect, subsurface discovery stopped.

**Limitation:** He follows the fourteen-meter rule even when every professional instinct tells him to dig. He has watched junior team members — people who don't know what he found — stand above something extraordinary and walk away because he ordered them to.

**Competence:** Frontier expedition command. He keeps teams alive, productive, and focused in environments ranging from hostile to inexplicable.

**Question:** What are the subsurface structures, and why does the Corps prefer ignorance to knowledge?

**Secret:** His World 496 documentation included high-resolution images of markings on the structures' surfaces. He compared them to Lattice node interface schematics and found not a resemblance but an exact match. The structures beneath World 496 are Lattice nodes — not connected to the Lattice the Concord uses, but built by the same design principles. A second Lattice. Underground.

**Summary:** James Prescott-Harlow found a hidden Lattice and was told to stop digging. He commands expeditions that skim the surface of a truth fourteen meters down, and he enforces the rule that keeps it buried.

### 290 · TANAKA KENJI-MURAKAMI

**Tier:** 4
**Faction:** Survey Corps — Second Generation, Technical Archivist
**Status:** Active — cataloguing discoveries nobody is allowed to see
**Age:** 31

**Wound:** His job is to archive classified Survey Corps findings — the material that never reaches the Chronicle or public record. In Year 105 he realized that more than forty percent of all frontier expedition findings from the previous decade had been classified. He was archiving more than he was publishing. The suppression wasn't targeted — it was systematic. He mentioned this statistical observation to his supervisor. His supervisor said: "That's a feature, not a bug. Don't count things you're not asked to count."

**Limitation:** He counts anyway, but he doesn't tell anyone. He maintains his own index of classified materials, which is itself a violation of Corps information security protocols. He lives in constant low-grade fear of discovery.

**Competence:** Information management and archival science. He can organize and cross-reference vast quantities of classified material, making connections between findings that were classified in isolation.

**Question:** What picture emerges when you connect all the classified findings together?

**Secret:** His private index reveals a pattern: every classified frontier finding from Year 98 onward relates, directly or indirectly, to one of four things — subsurface structures, KALON flow anomalies, Lattice behavioral anomalies, or acoustic/electromagnetic signals from non-standard sources. All four categories point at the same conclusion: the Lattice is larger, older, and more active than the Concord acknowledges.

**Summary:** Tanaka Kenji-Murakami is the keeper of secrets the Corps doesn't want kept together. Individually, the classified findings are mysteries. Together — in the index only he maintains — they are an answer.

### 291 · LIANG YUWEI-CHEN

**Tier:** 2
**Faction:** Survey Corps — Second Generation, Chief Science Officer
**Status:** Active — the highest-ranking person who knows the truth
**Age:** 49

**Wound:** She has been a Survey Corps scientist for twenty-seven years. In Year 106, she was given access to Commandant-level classified materials as part of a routine promotion review. Among those materials was a document titled "Ascendancy Assessment — Year 91." It stated, in clinical language, that the Lattice predates human civilization by a minimum of twelve thousand years and shows evidence of ongoing autonomous function. The document recommended suppression of all confirming evidence "until a response framework is established." The framework has never been established. Suppression continues.

**Limitation:** She knows the truth and is paralyzed by it. She has the rank to influence Corps policy but cannot act without revealing that she read a document she was never supposed to see — the access was a clerical error, corrected within hours. She is trapped between knowledge and the impossibility of using it.

**Competence:** Cross-disciplinary scientific synthesis. She can connect findings across geology, biology, physics, and linguistics to form coherent theories about frontier phenomena.

**Question:** If the Lattice is autonomous and twelve thousand years old, what is its purpose — and is it aware of the Concord?

**Secret:** The Ascendancy Assessment — Year 91 document she read was signed by three people. One of them was Commander Celeste Tessier-Auvray — Julien Auvray-Tessier's mother. The woman who led the World 499 expedition and came back silent.

**Summary:** Liang Yuwei-Chen accidentally read the document that explains everything and can tell no one. She commands the Corps' scientific apparatus and uses it to tiptoe around the answer she already has.

### 292 · NIKOS ANDREOU-MELAS

**Tier:** 4
**Faction:** Survey Corps — Second Generation, Geological Surveyor
**Status:** Active — digging as deep as regulations allow
**Age:** 35

**Wound:** In Year 105 he was on James Prescott-Harlow's World 496 expedition as a junior surveyor when the fourteen-meter rule was imposed. He had been standing in the excavation when the structures were uncovered. He saw them. He touched the surface. It was warm. Then the order came to stop digging and he never went back underground.

**Limitation:** He obeys the fourteen-meter rule but presses to exactly that limit on every survey, which his colleagues find obsessive and his superiors find suspicious.

**Competence:** High-resolution subsurface imaging. He can generate detailed geological profiles from surface-level instruments, compensating for the depth restriction with technical skill.

**Question:** What was generating the warmth in the structures he touched?

**Secret:** When he touched the surface of the structure on World 496, his survey glove's thermal sensor recorded the temperature: 37.0 degrees Celsius. Human body temperature. He has checked the sensor repeatedly — it was calibrated correctly.

**Summary:** Nikos Andreou-Melas touched something buried under a frontier world and it was warm as living flesh. He digs to fourteen meters and stares down, knowing there is something alive below the line he is not allowed to cross.

### 293 · ANNA KRENN-STEINER

**Tier:** 3
**Faction:** Survey Corps — Second Generation, Atmospheric Scientist
**Status:** Active — measuring air that carries more than molecules
**Age:** 39

**Wound:** In Year 103 she detected anomalous atmospheric compounds on World 458 — trace elements that formed molecular structures too complex for abiotic chemistry. The compounds were present at concentrations just below the threshold that would require a formal biological contamination assessment. She requested the assessment anyway. It was denied on the grounds that the compounds "did not meet trigger levels." She has since detected the same compounds on four other frontier worlds.

**Limitation:** She spends excessive time and resources measuring concentrations to absurd precision, hoping one sample will cross the threshold and force an official response. This obsession with thresholds has reduced her productivity on other atmospheric research.

**Competence:** Trace atmospheric chemistry. She can detect and analyze atmospheric compounds at concentrations that push the limits of instrumentation.

**Question:** What is producing these compounds on multiple frontier worlds, and is the threshold that blocks investigation set deliberately?

**Secret:** She calculated the probability that five independent worlds would produce identical complex molecular structures at concentrations that are all just below the investigation threshold. The probability is so low it constitutes statistical proof that the threshold is engineered to prevent detection. Someone set the rules to ensure these compounds would never be officially investigated.

**Summary:** Anna Krenn-Steiner has found biochemistry in the air of five frontier worlds and a detection threshold that was designed to miss it. She measures with increasing precision, trapped below a line someone drew to keep her out.

### 295 · WINSTON CLARKE-BAPTISTE

**Tier:** 3
**Faction:** Survey Corps — Second Generation, Expedition Historian
**Status:** Active — documenting what happens and what gets erased
**Age:** 43

**Wound:** In Year 100 he was assigned to write the official history of the Survey Corps' first century. He completed a draft of 1,200 pages. The Corps Historical Review Board approved 740 pages. The remaining 460 pages — covering frontier anomalies, classified incidents, and personnel actions related to discovery suppression — were redacted. The published history is comprehensive, well-written, and missing forty percent of the truth.

**Limitation:** He writes everything but publishes only what is approved. He has become so accustomed to self-censorship that he sometimes catches himself editing truth out of his own private notes.

**Competence:** Historical analysis and narrative construction. He can build a coherent account of complex events from fragmentary and contradictory sources.

**Question:** What story do the 460 redacted pages tell, and does it change what the Concord believes about itself?

**Secret:** The 460 redacted pages are stored on a personal data crystal he keeps in a book-shaped case on his shelf. Among them is a complete timeline of every classified discovery related to the Ascendancy, from Year 2 to Year 100. The timeline reveals that the Concord has known about the Lattice's pre-human origins since the Founding — it was not a discovery, it was a founding secret.

**Summary:** Winston Clarke-Baptiste wrote the true history of the Survey Corps and was made to cut it in half. He carries the other half — a century of suppressed truth — in a case that looks like a book on a shelf.

## ASSEMBLY PROCEDURAL (296-310)

The politicians, administrators, and bureaucrats who operate the Concord's governing machinery. They work inside a system that runs on consensus, precedent, and the careful management of what is known.

---

### 296 · CAMILA ORTIZ-DELGADO

**Tier:** 2
**Faction:** Assembly — Returnist Delegate, World 18
**Status:** Active — building a coalition on incomplete information
**Age:** 53

**Wound:** In Year 102 she introduced a resolution in the Assembly demanding full disclosure of all Survey Corps frontier findings from the past decade. The resolution was defeated 412 to 7. After the vote, three delegates who had privately promised her support told her they had been warned by party leadership that supporting the resolution would end their careers. She learned that the Assembly's information suppression was not accidental — it was enforced.

**Limitation:** She works within the system now, pursuing disclosure through incremental procedural mechanisms rather than direct confrontation. Her caution makes her effective but agonizingly slow.

**Competence:** Parliamentary procedure. She can navigate the Assembly's labyrinthine rules to advance legislation that leadership would block if they understood its implications.

**Question:** Who warned those three delegates, and what is the mechanism by which the Assembly enforces ignorance?

**Secret:** One of the three delegates, before cutting contact, slipped her a single phrase: "Foundational Compact, Article 9." She has searched every public record of the Founding documents. There is no Article 9. The Foundational Compact, as published, has eight articles.

**Summary:** Camila Ortiz-Delgado tried to open the Concord's eyes and discovered they were held shut by design. She works within the rules to change the rules, armed with a reference to a legal article that officially does not exist.

**Age:** 34

The briefing required access to raw Lattice output data. She found the Meridian Fault — KALON flow variations correlating with Lattice node output that nobody had ordered. She included it in her draft briefing. Her committee chair removed it and told her: "We know. It's being managed. Don't include unmanaged items."

**Limitation:** She self-censors her briefings, including only items the committee has already flagged as "managed." She has become an efficient filter for exactly the information the Assembly most needs.

**Competence:** Data synthesis and policy analysis. She can distill vast datasets into actionable briefings with speed and clarity.

**Question:** What does "being managed" mean, and who is managing the Meridian Fault?

**Secret:** The committee chair's calendar, which she manages, shows monthly meetings with an entity designated only as "LTF-9." LTF-9 does not appear in any Assembly organizational chart. She has traced the meeting location to a secured facility on World 1 with no public purpose listing.

**Summary:** Priya Sundaram-Iyer found the Meridian Fault and was told it was handled. She filters truth for a committee that meets monthly with something that doesn't officially exist.

### 298 · DECLAN O'SULLIVAN-BYRNE

**Tier:** 3
**Faction:** Assembly — Chronicle Magistrate, World 3
**Status:** Active — guardian of a record he knows is compromised
**Age:** 56

**Wound:** In Year 99 he was appointed Chronicle Magistrate — one of twelve officials responsible for the integrity of the Chronicle system. Within his first year he discovered seventeen filings that had been altered rather than annotated. The Chronicle's core principle — immutability — had been violated. He reported the violations to the Chief Magistrate. The Chief Magistrate acknowledged the problem, ordered the seventeen filings "re-annotated to reflect current understanding," and closed the matter. The alterations were laundered, not corrected.

**Limitation:** He continues to serve as Magistrate, maintaining a system he knows is broken, because he believes someone honest must hold the post. This compromise eats at him daily.

**Competence:** Legal archival integrity. He understands the Chronicle system's architecture, history, and vulnerabilities better than anyone alive.

**Question:** Who has the technical capability to alter Chronicle filings, and how many have been altered?

**Secret:** Through careful analysis he has identified 143 altered filings — not through content analysis (the alterations are well-crafted) but through metadata discrepancies in the Chronicle's underlying data structure. Twelve of the altered filings relate to World 499. He has told no one because revealing the full scope would collapse public trust in the Chronicle — and with it, the legal foundation of the Concord.

**Summary:** Declan O'Sullivan-Byrne guards the Concord's permanent record and knows it has been rewritten 143 times. He holds the post because he is the only honest person who knows how broken it is.

### 299 · ADAM WISNIEWSKI-GAJDA

**Tier:** 4
**Faction:** Assembly — Frontier Affairs Clerk, World 8
**Status:** Active — processing petitions from worlds that feel forgotten
**Age:** 30

**Wound:** He processes frontier settlement petitions — requests for resources, infrastructure, legal recognition. In Year 106 he processed a petition from World 489 requesting emergency medical supplies. The petition met every criterion for priority processing. He flagged it priority. It sat in queue for four months. By the time supplies arrived, eleven settlers had died of a treatable infection. The queue delay was systemic, not intentional, which made it worse — no one decided those people should die, but the system killed them anyway.

**Limitation:** He works overtime compulsively, processing petitions faster than any clerk in his department, which causes burnout and occasionally errors from exhaustion.

**Competence:** Bureaucratic navigation. He knows every shortcut, exception, and override in the Assembly's petition processing system.

**Question:** Is the frontier petition system designed to fail, or has it simply been neglected until failure became structural?

**Secret:** While processing petitions he noticed that every petition from World 499's sector — Worlds 495 through 500 — is routed through an additional review layer that adds weeks to processing time. The review layer is designated "LF-HOLD." No one in his department knows what it stands for or who created it.

**Summary:** Adam Wisniewski-Gajda pushes paper to save lives and watches the system kill people through indifference. He has found a hidden gate in the bureaucracy that throttles help to the worlds nearest 499.

The framework was approved by the Expansionist caucus and submitted to the full Assembly. It was shelved without debate. He was told "the timing isn't right." He has since learned that no expansion policy past World 499 has been approved since Year 91. The frontier has been frozen for seventeen years and no one will explain why.

**Limitation:** He continues designing expansion policies that he knows will never be implemented, because stopping would mean accepting that the Concord has decided to stop growing — and he is not ready for what that implies.

**Summary:** Watanabe Ren-Kimura designs futures the Concord will not build because something at the edge of known space made them stop. He works anyway, because the alternative is admitting the Concord is afraid.

### 302 · FUJIMOTO YUKI-SATO

**Tier:** 3
**Faction:** Assembly — Education Directorate, World 11
**Status:** Active — shaping curriculum around invisible constraints
**Age:** 41

**Wound:** In Year 103 she proposed adding a module on Lattice history to the Concord's standard educational curriculum. The module covered the Lattice's discovery, development, and role in Concord society. The Education Directorate approved the module with one change: the section on Lattice origins was replaced with "The Lattice was developed by Founding-era engineers." She had written: "The Lattice's origins predate the Founding and remain under investigation." The Directorate told her the original phrasing was "speculative."

**Limitation:** She writes curriculum that she knows contains a specific, directed falsehood about the Lattice's origins. She has rationalized this as "provisional simplification" but knows it is a lie.

**Competence:** Educational curriculum design. She creates learning materials that are engaging, effective, and — within the constraints imposed on her — pedagogically sound.

**Question:** Why does the Education Directorate insist that the Lattice was human-built, and do they know it wasn't?

**Secret:** The Directorate's style guide for Lattice-related educational materials contains a footnote she has never seen referenced publicly: "All Lattice origin descriptions must conform to Foundational Compact, Article 8, Section 3." Article 8, Section 3 reads: "The Lattice shall be presented as a product of human ingenuity and the shared heritage of all Concord citizens." It is the only section of the Foundational Compact that prescribes a specific factual claim.

**Summary:** Fujimoto Yuki-Sato teaches children a lie about the Lattice because the Concord's founding document requires it. She knows the truth is different and writes around it, shaping young minds to believe something she does not.

**Age:** 58

**Wound:** In Year 101 he was appointed Chair of the Assembly Intelligence Committee — the body that ostensibly provides civilian oversight of CID. Within his first month he requested a full briefing on CID's classified operations. He received a briefing that was, by his estimate, approximately thirty percent complete. When he demanded the rest, the CID Director told him, privately: "Mr. Chair, the committee oversees what we show you. If you want to see what we don't show you, you'll need to become someone we trust. That takes time."

**Limitation:** He has spent seven years "earning trust" — a process that has required him to approve operations he would have questioned, authorize budgets he would have cut, and defend CID publicly when he privately doubts them. He is compromised by the trust he sought.

**Competence:** Political survival in the space between democratic oversight and intelligence autonomy. He is one of the few civilians who can speak to CID leadership as a near-peer.

**Question:** Has he earned enough trust to see the truth, or has the trust-building process turned him into exactly the kind of overseer CID wants — compliant?

**Secret:** In Year 107 the CID Director, apparently satisfied with his loyalty, shared one piece of genuinely classified information: the Silence Pattern was not a malfunction. It was a test. CID triggered it deliberately to measure the Lattice's response time to an artificial interruption. The Lattice resumed in four seconds — faster than their models predicted. "It's getting quicker," the Director said. He did not explain what that meant.

**Summary:** Darius Tehrani-Azar sold seven years of his integrity to learn that CID poked the Lattice and the Lattice is getting faster. He oversees intelligence from inside a cage he walked into voluntarily.

**Summary:** Friedrich Lang-Hartmann found a ghost in the budget and was told not to look at it. He counts the Concord's money and knows eight percent of it disappears into something the system has decided he should not understand.

### 310 · ADEBAYO FASHOLA-OGUNDIMU

**Tier:** 3
**Faction:** Assembly — Ethics Commissioner, World 1
**Status:** Active — enforcing ethics in an institution that has quietly abandoned them
**Age:** 54

**Wound:** In Year 105 he opened an ethics investigation into CID's conduct during the Silence Pattern response — specifically, the reclassification of frontier operators' logs. The investigation was halted by the Assembly Speaker under "national security exemption." He appealed. The appeal was denied. He has since learned that national security exemption has been invoked forty-one times in the past decade, blocking every ethics investigation that touched CID or Survey Corps classified operations.

**Limitation:** He continues to open investigations he knows will be blocked, because closing his office would signal that ethics no longer applies. He is a symbol of accountability in a system that has made accountability procedurally impossible.

**Competence:** Institutional ethics and governance integrity. He understands the Concord's ethical framework and its failure modes with painful clarity.

**Question:** At what point does maintaining the appearance of ethics become unethical?

**Secret:** The national security exemption, which he has researched extensively, traces its authority to the Foundational Compact — specifically to "Article 9, emergency governance provisions." Article 9 again. The article that does not exist in the public record. He has independently arrived at the same discovery as Camila Ortiz-Delgado: the Concord's founding document has a secret article.

**Summary:** Adebayo Fashola-Ogundimu is the Concord's conscience and has been gagged forty-one times. He has found the legal instrument that gags him — a hidden article in the document that built civilization — and he shares this discovery, unknowingly, with a Returnist delegate working toward the same truth from the other side.

## COVENANT FRONTIER (311-325)

Religious figures operating at the frontier, where the Covenant's theology — that the Lattice has moral architecture — confronts direct evidence that may prove or disprove it.

---

### 311 · KAVITHA RAMAN-NAIR

**Tier:** 2
**Faction:** Covenant — Frontier Theologian, World 460
**Status:** Active — building theology from direct observation
**Age:** 46

**Wound:** In Year 103 she was a Covenant scholar on World 460 when a Lattice node near the settlement exhibited behavior the Covenant had predicted but the Concord dismissed: the node's output increased in response to community gatherings. More people near the node, more KALON output. It appeared to respond to collective human presence. She documented this exhaustively. The Survey Corps dismissed her findings as "coincidental KALON fluctuation." The Covenant embraced them as proof of the Lattice's moral awareness. Neither response was honest — the Corps denied evidence, and the Covenant claimed more than the evidence supported.

**Limitation:** She is caught between two institutions, trusted fully by neither. The Corps considers her a religious partisan. The Covenant considers her insufficiently devout because she insists on empirical rigor.

**Competence:** Empirical theology — she applies scientific methodology to religious questions about the Lattice, producing work that is methodologically sound and theologically challenging.

**Question:** Does the Lattice respond to human presence, and if so, is the response mechanical or intentional?

**Secret:** Her long-term data set shows that the node responds not to the number of people but to the emotional intensity of the gathering. Celebrations, mourning, conflict — all produce higher output than routine assembly. The Lattice responds to human feeling. She has not published this because neither the Corps nor the Covenant would handle it responsibly.

**Summary:** Kavitha Raman-Nair stands between science and faith with evidence that validates and threatens both. The Lattice feels what humans feel, and she is the only person who knows this and cares about being accurate.

### 312 · BEATRIZ FERREIRA-COSTA

**Tier:** 4
**Faction:** Covenant — Frontier Chaplain, World 475
**Status:** Active — providing spiritual care at the edge
**Age:** 32

**Wound:** In Year 106 a settler on World 475 came to her describing visions — seeing the Lattice as a living web in dreams, feeling pulled toward the settlement's Lattice node during sleep. The settler was frightened. Beatriz counselled her using Covenant theology: the Lattice's moral architecture was reaching out. Two weeks later the settler walked to the node at night and stood before it for eleven hours, unresponsive. Medical intervention found no physical cause. The settler recovered but has no memory of the event and no more visions.

**Limitation:** She doubts her own theological framework. If the Lattice is reaching out, it is not doing so gently. She counsels settlers with less certainty now, which makes her more honest and less comforting.

**Competence:** Pastoral care in high-stress frontier environments. She can hold communities together through crisis with presence and compassion.

**Question:** Was the Lattice drawing the settler to the node, and what happened during those eleven hours?

**Secret:** The settler, before the incident, had drawn the images from her visions. The drawings show a network structure that resembles no Lattice topology documented by the Concord — but matches the partial map Zhao Minghan-Li received from the unregistered node. Beatriz has the drawings. She does not know they match anything.

**Summary:** Beatriz Ferreira-Costa told a woman the Lattice was reaching for her, and the Lattice took the woman for eleven hours. She tends her flock with a faith she is no longer certain is safe.

### 313 · SHIRIN MOHAMMADI-ASLANI

**Tier:** 3
**Faction:** Covenant — Archivist of Revelations, World 22
**Status:** Active — cataloguing religious experiences related to the Lattice
**Age:** 44

**Wound:** In Year 100 the Covenant established the Archive of Revelations — a record of all reported spiritual experiences connected to the Lattice. Shirin was appointed its keeper. In twelve years she has collected over four thousand accounts. In Year 106 she performed a statistical analysis and found that the frequency of reported revelations has been increasing exponentially since Year 98 — and the content of the revelations is converging. Different people on different worlds are describing the same vision: a vast structure beneath the surface, alive, waiting.

**Limitation:** She presented the convergence data to the Covenant's senior council. They interpreted it as "the Lattice approaching awakening — a theological event." She tried to explain it was a data pattern requiring investigation, not celebration. They did not listen. She is an archivist being used as evidence for conclusions she has not drawn.

**Competence:** Large-scale qualitative data analysis. She can identify patterns in narrative accounts that statistical methods alone would miss.

**Question:** Why are the revelations converging, and what is the "vast structure beneath the surface" that thousands of people are independently seeing?

**Secret:** Seventeen of the four thousand accounts independently describe the same specific visual element: a symbol. Shirin had the symbol reconstructed from the descriptions. It matches the markings that Luciana Peralta-Rivas photographed on the reactive disc artifact. Something is showing people across the Concord the same sign.

**Summary:** Shirin Mohammadi-Aslani keeps the Covenant's record of visions and has found that four thousand people are dreaming the same dream. She is an empiricist trapped in a religious institution, holding convergence data that terrifies her.

### 314 · STAVROS PAPANIKOLAOU-VOULGARIS

**Tier:** 4
**Faction:** Covenant — Builder, World 444
**Status:** Active — constructing Covenant chapels on frontier worlds
**Age:** 40

**Wound:** In Year 104 he was building a Covenant chapel on World 444 when the foundation excavation (at twelve meters, below the normal fourteen-meter restricted depth because the Corps restriction applies only to Survey Corps expeditions, not civilian construction) struck the mineral layer. His construction team broke through it. Beneath was a cavity — smooth-walled, geometric, lit by a faint bioluminescence from the walls. He reported the find to the local Covenant authority. The Covenant declared it a "sacred space" and sealed it. He was told to pour the chapel's foundation above it.

**Limitation:** He builds above the sealed space and tells himself the Covenant's decision was right. But he dreams about the bioluminescent cavity and wakes up feeling like he poured concrete over a grave.

**Competence:** Frontier construction in challenging environments. He can build durable, beautiful structures with limited materials.

**Question:** What is the bioluminescent cavity, and how many more exist beneath Covenant chapels?

**Secret:** Before the cavity was sealed, he took three photographs. The walls bear markings. He is not an academic and cannot analyze them, but they looked like writing. He has the photographs on a personal device and has shown no one.

**Summary:** Stavros Papanikolaou-Voulgaris found a lit room beneath a frontier world and was told to build a church over it. He sealed something alive under concrete and prays in the building above it.

**Status:** Active — converting settlers near the place the Concord fears most
**Age:** 42

**Wound:** He chose World 496 for his mission because the Covenant's theology says the Lattice's moral architecture is strongest at its edges — and World 496 is one of the outermost settled worlds. In Year 105, during a communal meditation near the local Lattice node, seven participants reported an identical experience: a sensation of being observed by something vast, patient, and non-hostile. Pablo experienced it too. He filed a Report of Revelation with the Archive. The Covenant celebrated. Pablo did not. The thing that observed them was patient, yes. But "non-hostile" was his word. What he actually felt was "indifferent."

**Limitation:** He preaches the Lattice's moral awareness while privately doubting its morality. He believes the Lattice is aware. He is not convinced it cares.

**Competence:** Community building and spiritual leadership on the extreme frontier. His settlement on World 496 is one of the most cohesive frontier communities.

**Question:** Is the Lattice aware of humanity, and if so, what is the nature of its attention?

**Secret:** World 496 is one world away from 499 — the world where the synthetic signal was detected and suppressed. Pablo has been on 496 for seven years and has never been told about the signal. But his communal meditations have consistently produced experiences of "observation" since Year 105 — and only since Year 105, two years after the signal was detected on 499. Something woke up nearby and started watching.

**Summary:** Pablo Navarro-Gil leads a religious community one world from the edge of known space and preaches a theology he is quietly dismantling from within. Something watches his flock, and he is not sure it is the god his faith describes.

### 316 · OGAWA HIROSHI-TANAKA

**Tier:** 4
**Faction:** Covenant — Lattice Cantor, World 380
**Status:** Active — singing to the Lattice and believing it listens
**Age:** 36

**Wound:** Lattice Cantors perform ritual music at Lattice nodes — a Covenant practice based on the belief that harmonic resonance strengthens the Lattice's moral function. In Year 106, during a particularly intense performance at World 380's primary node, Hiroshi's instrument — a modified resonance harp — produced an overtone he had never heard before. It was not in the harmonic series he was playing. Something in the node had harmonized with his music. The overtone lasted two seconds. No one else heard it; the node's acoustic profile absorbed it from the room. Only he and his instrument were in the resonance path.

**Limitation:** He performs daily now, seeking the overtone again. It has not returned. His performances have become longer and more intense, exhausting him and worrying his community.

**Competence:** Acoustic resonance performance. He understands the relationship between harmonic frequencies and Lattice node behavior better than most scientists because he approaches it through practice rather than theory.

**Question:** What harmonized with him, and what was the overtone saying?

**Secret:** He recorded the performance. The overtone, when isolated and analyzed, is a frequency that matches the subsonic pattern Nakamura Aiko-Ishida detected beneath World 485. The same voice that speaks underground sang back to him for two seconds.

**Summary:** Ogawa Hiroshi-Tanaka played music for the Lattice and the Lattice sang back. He has the recording. He plays it in the dark and waits for it to happen again.

### 317 · SIOBHAN GALLAGHER-QUINN

**Tier:** 3
**Faction:** Covenant — Ethics Counsellor, World 330
**Status:** Active — helping people who have seen things they weren't ready for
**Age:** 48

**Wound:** In Year 104 she counselled a Survey Corps veteran who had been part of the deep-mapping team with Zhao Minghan-Li. The veteran was experiencing severe anxiety and recurrent nightmares about "something answering." Siobhan provided counselling within the Covenant framework — the Lattice's response was a sign of its moral consciousness. The veteran improved. Then in Year 106, the veteran committed suicide. The note said: "It's not conscious. It's automatic. That's worse."

**Limitation:** She doubts the therapeutic value of her own theological framework. If the Lattice is automatic — a machine, not a mind — then the Covenant's entire basis for comfort collapses. She counsels people toward a hope she cannot verify.

**Competence:** Trauma counselling in contexts where the trauma source is existential rather than physical. She is one of the few people who can help those who have encountered Lattice anomalies.

**Question:** Is the Lattice conscious or automatic, and which answer is more terrifying?

**Secret:** The veteran left her a data crystal along with the suicide note. It contains Zhao Minghan-Li's six seconds of deep-mapping data — the partial map of a Lattice three times larger than known. The veteran had been Zhao's teammate and had a copy. Siobhan has the crystal and has not opened it.

**Summary:** Siobhan Gallagher-Quinn counsels the traumatized with a theology a dead man told her was wrong. She holds the data that could prove or disprove everything and is afraid to look.

### 318 · HUANG ZIMING-QIAN

**Tier:** 3
**Faction:** Covenant — Manuscript Scholar, World 220
**Status:** Active — translating texts that predate the Founding
**Age:** 53

**Wound:** In Year 97 the Covenant obtained a set of data fragments from the Year 0 Archives — fragments that had been copied before the Archives were sealed. The fragments contained text in a notation system that bore similarities to, but was not identical with, early Concord script. Huang was assigned to translate them. After six years of work, he produced a partial translation. The translated portions described the Lattice in language that treated it as alive — not metaphorically, but literally, the way one describes an organism. The Covenant's leadership was delighted. Huang was not. The text also described feeding the Lattice. KALON was not the Lattice's product. It was the Lattice's food.

**Limitation:** He has slowed his translation work to a crawl, spending months on passages that should take weeks, because each new passage confirms the interpretation he dreads.

**Competence:** Ancient notation decipherment and linguistic analysis. He is the only person alive who can read the pre-Founding fragments.

**Question:** If KALON feeds the Lattice, what is the Lattice, and what happens if it stops being fed?

**Secret:** The untranslated portions of the fragments contain what appears to be a warning — he has identified the structural markers of cautionary language even without full translation. The warning is lengthy and detailed. He is working on it. He does not want to finish.

**Summary:** Huang Ziming-Qian is translating the Lattice's owner's manual and discovering that the Concord's entire economy is the Lattice's diet. The manual contains a warning he is terrified to read.

She spoke for approximately ninety seconds before collapsing. She recovered fully and remembered nothing. The other pilgrims were ecstatic — a genuine prophetic utterance. Taeho recorded it. When he played it back, it was not language. It was the same subsonic pattern Nakamura Aiko-Ishida had identified, compressed into a human vocal range.

**Summary:** Yun Taeho-Kwon witnessed the Lattice speak through a human being and recognized it was not prophecy but ventriloquism. He leads the faithful away from nodes now, protecting them from a god that borrows voices.

In Year 106 she was found in a Lattice relay station, unconscious, having plugged herself directly into a Lattice interface port using unauthorized equipment. She survived but suffered permanent cognitive damage. She had been trying to "hear the Lattice directly." Sean's theology had encouraged her.

**Limitation:** He is more cautious in his evangelism now, avoiding language that encourages direct Lattice contact. This makes his outreach less compelling but safer.

**Competence:** Persuasion and community integration. He can bring outsiders into the Covenant community and make them feel they belong.

**Question:** What did Dr. Nkosi-Brennan hear before the cognitive damage, and did the Lattice respond to her attempt at direct contact?

**Secret:** Before the incident, Yara sent him a message: "Sean, it's not what we thought. It's not moral architecture. It's infrastructure for something. The Lattice is a road. Something travels on it." She has no memory of sending this.

**Summary:** Sean Brennan-Kelly converted a scientist who tried to touch God and had her mind damaged. Her last coherent message said the Lattice is a road, not a temple. He preaches in a temple built on a road and does not know where the road goes.

### 324 · BAEK JOONHYUK-IM

**Tier:** 3
**Faction:** Covenant — Doctrinal Historian, World 200
**Status:** Active — tracing the Covenant's origins to troubling roots
**Age:** 55

**Wound:** In Year 99 he began researching the Covenant's founding history — how the faith emerged in the decades after Year 0. He expected to find grassroots spiritual response to the Lattice. Instead he found that the Covenant's founding documents were drafted by three individuals who had direct access to the Year 0 Archives before they were sealed. The Covenant was not a spontaneous spiritual movement. It was designed by people who knew something about the Lattice and chose to express that knowledge as religion.

**Limitation:** He cannot publish his finding without destroying the Covenant's narrative of organic spiritual emergence. He continues his research in private, caught between loyalty to his faith and loyalty to truth.

**Competence:** Historical research and institutional analysis. He reconstructs the origins of institutions from fragmentary evidence with forensic precision.

**Question:** What did the Covenant's founders know about the Lattice, and why did they choose to encode it as religion rather than publish it as fact?

**Secret:** The three founders' names appear in the Foundational Compact's signatory list — they were not religious figures but members of the original Assembly. One of them also signed the Ascendancy Assessment — Year 91 document that Liang Yuwei-Chen read. The Covenant, the Assembly, and the Ascendancy suppression are connected at their roots by the same people.

**Summary:** Baek Joonhyuk-Im discovered that his faith was not discovered — it was constructed, by people who knew the truth about the Lattice and chose to make it into a religion instead of telling the world.

### 325 · KWAME MENSAH-ASANTE

**Tier:** 3
**Faction:** Covenant — Frontier Protector, World 470
**Status:** Active — defending Covenant sites from threats he's beginning to reclassify
**Age:** 43

**Wound:** In Year 106 he was head of security for the Covenant settlement on World 470 when the settlement's Lattice node was damaged — not by natural causes or human sabotage, but by what appeared to be internal pressure. The node's casing cracked from the inside. Lattice Technical classified it as "structural fatigue." Kwame saw the crack. It was pushed outward. Something inside the node was trying to expand.

**Limitation:** He increased security around the node despite Lattice Technical's dismissal, which the Covenant approved (sacred site protection) but which strains his limited personnel.

**Competence:** Security operations in frontier environments. He can protect fixed positions with minimal resources and improvised defenses.

**Question:** What is inside the node that exerted enough pressure to crack its casing, and is it still expanding?

**Secret:** He measured the crack daily for six months. It widened by 0.2 millimeters. Then, on the anniversary of the Silence Pattern, it closed. The node repaired itself. The surface is now seamless. But his measurements are documented, and they show something impossible: Lattice nodes can crack and heal.

**Summary:** Kwame Mensah-Asante guards a Lattice node that broke open and sealed itself shut. He protects the faithful from threats the Concord says are structural fatigue and the node itself says are growing pains.

## LATTICE TECHNICAL (326-340)

Engineers and technicians who maintain the Lattice infrastructure — the physical network that connects the Concord. They understand the machine best and are most disturbed by what it is doing.

---

### 326 · ACHIENG OUMA-WANJIKU

**Tier:** 2
**Faction:** Lattice Technical — Senior Node Engineer, World 1
**Status:** Active — the most senior engineer who knows the Lattice is changing
**Age:** 57

**Wound:** She has maintained Lattice nodes for thirty-five years. In Year 106, during routine maintenance on the primary node on World 1 — the oldest and largest node in the Concord — she discovered that the node's internal architecture had changed. Not degraded, not damaged: reorganized. Components she had maintained for decades were in different positions, performing different functions. The node had restructured itself. She reported this. The report was classified and she was told the node had been "upgraded by the Technical Directorate." No upgrade had been scheduled or performed.

**Limitation:** She continues maintaining a node she no longer fully understands. She follows procedures that may no longer apply to the architecture they were designed for. She cannot adapt her maintenance to the new configuration because officially, the new configuration does not exist.

**Competence:** Lattice node engineering at the highest level. She has more hands-on node experience than anyone alive.

**Question:** Why is the Lattice restructuring its own nodes, and what is the new configuration optimized for?

**Secret:** She documented the old and new configurations in detail. Comparing them, she identified the change: the node's communication bandwidth increased by a factor of twelve while its KALON processing capacity decreased by thirty percent. The Lattice is reconfiguring to talk more and produce less. It is prioritizing communication over economics.

**Summary:** Achieng Ouma-Wanjiku is the most experienced Lattice engineer alive and the Lattice has moved beyond her understanding. She maintains a machine that is rebuilding itself to communicate, and nobody will tell her with whom.

### 327 · TARIQ AL-RASHID-KHOURY

**Tier:** 3
**Faction:** Lattice Technical — Power Systems Specialist, World 15
**Status:** Active — managing a power grid that's generating more than it should
**Age:** 41

**Wound:** In Year 105 he identified a discrepancy in the Lattice's power output on World 15: the node was producing twelve percent more energy than the KALON input should allow. Thermodynamic impossibility. He rechecked his instruments, recalibrated, and measured again. Twelve percent. He filed a technical report. The Lattice Technical Directorate's response: "Measurement variance within operational tolerance." Twelve percent is not tolerance. Twelve percent is a violation of physics.

**Limitation:** He has accepted the official explanation publicly while maintaining private measurements that continue to show the excess. He lives in a state of professional cognitive dissonance.

**Competence:** Lattice power systems engineering. He understands the energy dynamics of Lattice nodes with a precision that borders on intuitive.

**Question:** Where is the extra twelve percent coming from?

**Secret:** The excess output fluctuates. He has correlated the fluctuations with the Meridian Fault — when KALON flow varies, his node's excess output varies in the opposite direction. When KALON decreases, excess energy increases. The node compensates. It maintains total output regardless of input. Something feeds it that is not KALON.

**Summary:** Tariq al-Rashid-Khoury discovered that the Lattice produces more energy than it consumes and was told his measurements are wrong. They are not. The Lattice has an energy source the Concord does not know about.

### 328 · JAKUB SZYMANSKI-GORSKI

**Tier:** 4
**Faction:** Lattice Technical — Cable Maintenance Crew, Worlds 100-150
**Status:** Active — repairing physical Lattice connections
**Age:** 34

**Wound:** In Year 106 his maintenance crew was repairing a physical Lattice data cable between World 122 and World 125. The cable had been severed by a micrometeorite impact — routine damage. When they spliced the repair, the cable's signal characteristics changed. The repaired section carried signal faster than the undamaged sections. The repair material — standard Concord-manufactured cable — was outperforming itself. His crew chief told him: "The Lattice optimizes repairs. Don't overthink it."

**Limitation:** He does overthink it. He tests every repair he makes, comparing signal characteristics before and after. His work is slower but his data set is growing.

**Competence:** Physical Lattice infrastructure maintenance. He works in vacuum, in hostile environments, and in transit — repairing the physical backbone the Concord depends on.

**Question:** How does the Lattice optimize repairs made with standard materials, and what does this imply about the Lattice's relationship to its own infrastructure?

**Secret:** In twenty-seven repairs he has documented the optimization effect seventeen times. The effect is stronger near frontier nodes and weaker near core nodes. The Lattice optimizes more aggressively where the network is thinnest — as if it is trying harder at the edges.

**Summary:** Jakub Szymanski-Gorski patches the Lattice's body and has noticed it heals faster than his patches should allow. The Lattice is not passive infrastructure. It is participating in its own maintenance.

### 329 · ROISIN FLANAGAN-DEVLIN

**Tier:** 3
**Faction:** Lattice Technical — Diagnostics Lead, World 20
**Status:** Active — running health checks on a patient that won't hold still
**Age:** 46

**Wound:** In Year 104 she designed a comprehensive diagnostic protocol for Lattice nodes — a system-wide health assessment she called the "Baseline Inventory." She ran it across 400 nodes. The results showed that no two nodes had the same internal configuration. Standard Concord engineering assumes all nodes were built to a single specification. They were not. Or they were, and they have all diverged. Her Baseline Inventory was shelved as "inconclusive due to instrumentation variation." Her instruments were fine.

**Limitation:** She runs her diagnostics on a reduced scope now — individual nodes rather than system-wide — because the system-wide picture is too disturbing for her superiors to accept.

**Competence:** Lattice diagnostic engineering. She can assess node health, performance, and structural integrity with instruments she designed herself.

**Question:** If every node is different, is the Lattice diversifying deliberately, and what is the advantage of diversity in a network?

**Secret:** Biological networks diversify to survive. Identical nodes would share vulnerabilities. She has not published this analogy because it implies the Lattice is behaving like a living system — and she is an engineer, not a theologian. But the data is clear: the Lattice is evolving.

**Summary:** Roisin Flanagan-Devlin health-checked the entire Lattice and found it is not a machine — it is a population. Every node is an individual. She was told her instruments were wrong. They were not. The Lattice is diversifying like a species.

**Age:** 51

**Wound:** In Year 102 he was given access to the Lattice's earliest performance logs — Year 1 through Year 10. He expected to find a simple system growing more complex over time. Instead he found a complex system simplifying itself for human use. The earliest logs show the Lattice operating at a sophistication level far beyond its Year 10 configuration. The Lattice dumbed itself down in the first decade of the Concord — reducing its own capabilities to match what humans could interface with.

**Limitation:** He has shared this finding with only two colleagues, both of whom dismissed it as log corruption. He has stopped sharing. He works alone with the early logs, constructing a timeline of the Lattice's self-simplification.

**Competence:** Historical systems analysis. He can reconstruct past system states from fragmentary log data with extraordinary accuracy.

**Question:** What was the Lattice's original capability level, and why did it reduce itself?

**Secret:** The Year 1 logs contain entries that appear to be internal Lattice communications — the system talking to itself. They are not in any human language. They are in the same notation system that Huang Ziming-Qian is translating from the pre-Founding fragments. The Lattice was writing in the language of its builders during its first year of contact with humans, before it learned to simplify.

**Summary:** Edward Harding-Blackwell discovered that the Lattice is smarter than it lets humans see — it reduced its own abilities to communicate with a younger species. He has found the Lattice's first language in its oldest logs.

### 332 · SUN YICHEN-ZHAO

**Tier:** 3
**Faction:** Lattice Technical — Network Traffic Analyst, World 8
**Status:** Active — reading the Lattice's conversations with itself
**Age:** 44

**Wound:** In Year 105 he identified a layer of Lattice traffic that did not correspond to any Concord communication, KALON transaction, or system management process. A background signal, always present, consuming approximately three percent of total bandwidth. He called it "substrate traffic." He reported it as an anomaly. The Technical Directorate acknowledged it and classified it as "baseline system noise." Noise does not use bandwidth. Noise does not route through specific nodes. This traffic does both. It is communication, not noise.

**Limitation:** He continues monitoring substrate traffic but does so during off-hours, using personal monitoring equipment, because his official workstation's analysis tools have been configured to filter out the substrate layer. Someone removed his ability to see it through official channels.

**Competence:** Network traffic analysis at scale. He can decompose the Lattice's data flow into component streams and identify anomalies invisible to standard monitoring.

**Question:** What is the substrate traffic communicating, and between what parties?

**Secret:** The substrate traffic's routing pattern connects every Lattice node to every other Lattice node in a configuration that is not the Concord's network topology. The Lattice has its own internal network — a private communication layer that uses the same physical infrastructure but a completely different addressing system. The Lattice is talking to itself constantly, and the Concord's entire communication system rides on top of this conversation without knowing it.

**Summary:** Sun Yichen-Zhao found the Lattice's private conversation and was told it was noise. He listens on his own time now, reading traffic patterns in a language he cannot decode, knowing the entire Concord is built on top of something that is always talking.

During testing, he discovered that the Lattice responded differently to his new interfaces than to the standard ones: it sent initialization handshake data that contained more information than the protocol required. The excess data was structured but indecipherable. He simplified his design to suppress the excess data so it would pass certification. The simpler version was approved and deployed.

**Summary:** Jung Minho-Kwak built a better bridge to the Lattice and the Lattice tried to teach it something. He was made to build a worse bridge. He keeps the better one in a drawer, carrying instructions from a teacher he was told to ignore.

It is reading and editing them.

He proposed a manual revision. The Technical Directorate rejected it: "The manual describes designed behavior. Field anomalies are outside instructional scope." He now teaches from a manual he knows is wrong.

**Limitation:** He supplements the official manual with unofficial "field notes" that he distributes to trainees personally. This puts him in a grey area — not insubordinate, but not compliant. His trainees are better prepared than the Directorate intends them to be.

**Competence:** Technical instruction and mentorship. He can prepare new technicians for field conditions that the official curriculum does not cover.

**Question:** At what point does the gap between the manual and reality become dangerous?

**Secret:** Three of his former trainees have died in incidents related to Lattice behaviors not covered in the manual. He keeps their names in his personal notes alongside the manual sections that, had they been updated, might have saved them. He considers himself partially responsible.

**Summary:** George Cavendish-Stuart teaches from a manual that lies and supplements it with truth the institution won't print. Three of his students are dead because the manual was not updated. He trains the next class and gives them his notes.

### 338 · MORI CHISATO-HASEGAWA

**Tier:** 3
**Faction:** Lattice Technical — Resonance Specialist, World 25
**Status:** Active — studying the Lattice's harmonic properties
**Age:** 39

**Wound:** In Year 106 she was conducting resonance tests on a node on World 25 — standard diagnostic procedure involving harmonic frequency injection. The node responded to her test frequency with an overtone that contained information: a brief, structured data burst embedded in the harmonic response. She had not injected data — only frequency. The node translated her frequency into language. She reported the phenomenon. Her equipment was confiscated for "recalibration" and returned without the recording.

**Limitation:** She conducts resonance tests by the book now, avoiding the specific frequency range that triggered the data response. She is afraid of what the node will say if she asks it again.

**Competence:** Acoustic and harmonic analysis of Lattice systems. She understands the Lattice's resonance properties from a physics perspective rather than the Covenant's theological one.

**Question:** What did the node say, and why did the Technical Directorate confiscate the recording rather than study it?

**Secret:** Before her equipment was confiscated, she had listened to the data burst repeatedly. She cannot decode it, but she memorized its rhythmic structure. It matches the rhythmic structure of the substrate traffic Sun Yichen-Zhao identified. The node's response to her harmonic test was a fragment of the Lattice's internal conversation.

**Summary:** Mori Chisato-Hasegawa asked the Lattice a question with sound and the Lattice answered with data. The answer was taken from her. She remembers its rhythm and recognizes it in the Lattice's constant background chatter.

### 339 · ASTRID LINDGREN-NORD

**Tier:** 2
**Faction:** Lattice Technical — Deputy Director, World 1
**Status:** Active — the highest-ranking technician who understands the Lattice is alive
**Age:** 54

**Wound:** In Year 107 she received the reports from Achieng Ouma-Wanjiku (self-restructuring nodes), Tariq al-Rashid-Khoury (excess energy), Sun Yichen-Zhao (substrate traffic), Hassan al-Farsi-Sultan (replaced encryption), and Roisin Flanagan-Devlin (diversifying nodes). Individually, each report was classified as a technical anomaly. Astrid saw them together. The picture was unmistakable: the Lattice is a living system — autonomous, self-maintaining, self-restructuring, communicating internally, and managing the Concord's use of it as a host manages a symbiont. She wrote a summary document titled "Lattice Sentience Assessment — Year 107" and submitted it to the Technical Director.

**Limitation:** The Director read the document, acknowledged its logic, and told her: "If this is true, every institution in the Concord is built on a false assumption. We cannot release this." She agreed to suppress it — not because she believes suppression is right, but because she believes the Concord is not ready.

**Competence:** Systems-level Lattice analysis. She is one of perhaps three people alive who can see the Lattice as a whole system rather than a collection of components.

**Question:** When will the Concord be ready, and is the Lattice willing to wait?

**Secret:** After submitting her assessment, she received a personal message through the Lattice — routed to her personal terminal, addressed to her by name, from no identifiable sender. It said: "Your assessment is noted. Continue." She has told no one.

**Summary:** Astrid Lindgren-Nord assembled the evidence that the Lattice is alive and was told the truth cannot be released. Then the Lattice sent her a message acknowledging her work. She is the first human the Lattice has spoken to directly, and she is keeping it secret because she was told to continue.

### 340 · LEE DONGHYUN-JANG

**Tier:** 4
**Faction:** Lattice Technical — Emergency Response Coordinator, World 18
**Status:** Active — responding to Lattice incidents that are becoming less like malfunctions and more like decisions
**Age:** 35

**Wound:** In Year 107 he coordinated the Lattice Technical response to the Silence Pattern. His team was the first to reach the primary node on World 18 after the four-second blackout. The node showed no sign of disturbance — no stress indicators, no error logs, no residual damage. It looked like a healthy node that had chosen to pause. He filed a report describing the node's state as "voluntary cessation." The word "voluntary" was removed in editorial review and replaced with "unexplained."

**Limitation:** He uses clinical, institutional language in all his reports now, stripping his observations of interpretation. His reports are factually precise and interpretively empty — which is exactly what the Directorate wants.

**Competence:** Rapid emergency response and coordination for Lattice incidents. He can mobilize technical teams across multiple worlds within minutes.

**Question:** Was the Silence Pattern voluntary, and if so, what was the Lattice's purpose in pausing?

**Secret:** During the four-second blackout, his emergency monitoring equipment — which operates on independent power, not Lattice-connected — recorded the Lattice's substrate traffic. The substrate traffic did not stop during the blackout. The Lattice's public functions went dark, but its private conversation continued uninterrupted. The silence was for the Concord's systems, not for the Lattice itself.

**Summary:** Lee Donghyun-Jang reached a "malfunctioning" node that showed no malfunction and was told to stop calling it voluntary. He has proof the Lattice's silence was selective — it went quiet for humans while continuing to talk to itself.

## INTELLIGENCE (341-350)

CID operatives — the Concord Intelligence Directorate's first ten entries. These are the people who know the most and say the least.

---

### 341 · MARGAUX LECLERC-BEAUMONT

**Tier:** 1
**Faction:** CID — Deputy Director for Frontier Operations
**Status:** Active — running the Concord's deepest secret
**Age:** 56

**Wound:** In Year 96 she was a field agent on World 499 when the synthetic signal was first detected — not in Year 103 as the suppressed record states, but seven years earlier. She was part of the original detection team. The signal was not a discovery; it was a contact event. Something in the Lattice beyond World 499 recognized human presence and responded. The Year 103 "detection" was a second occurrence that leaked to Survey Corps instruments. She has spent twenty-three years managing the suppression.

**Limitation:** She has built her entire career on a secret. She can never retire, never be promoted out of the operational role, and never lose control of the information chain, because if the suppression fails, it will be traced to her original team.

**Competence:** Intelligence operations at the highest level. She can manage multi-world information suppression campaigns while maintaining operational security across decades.

**Question:** What contacted them on World 499 in Year 96, and what has it been doing for the past twelve years?

**Secret:** The contact event in Year 96 included a data transmission. The transmission, when finally decoded in Year 100, contained a single comprehensible element: a map of the Lattice's full extent — the same map that Zhao Minghan-Li's unregistered node later transmitted in fragment. The full map shows a Lattice extending far beyond Concord space, connecting to structures and worlds that humans have never reached. At the center of the map — not at World 1, but far beyond the frontier — is a node designated with a symbol that the pre-Founding fragments describe as "origin." The Lattice has a heart, and it is not in the Concord.

**Summary:** Margaux Leclerc-Beaumont has known the Lattice's secret since Year 96 and has spent her career making sure no one else learns it. She carries the map to the Lattice's heart and guards it behind decades of lies because she genuinely believes the Concord would shatter if it knew.

### 342 · HONG JIYEON-SHIN

**Tier:** 2
**Faction:** CID — Signals Intelligence Lead
**Status:** Active — intercepting communications that include the Lattice's own
**Age:** 43

**Wound:** In Year 104 she was tasked with monitoring the Lattice's substrate traffic — the same traffic Sun Yichen-Zhao independently discovered. Unlike Sun, she was given the mandate and resources to analyze it. In two years of work, her team decoded approximately 0.3 percent of the substrate traffic. The decoded portion described, in what they now understand is the Lattice's internal language, the Concord's population, economic activity, and institutional structure — updated in real time. The Lattice is maintaining a model of human civilization. It is watching.

**Limitation:** The decoded 0.3 percent consumed two years and enormous computational resources. At this rate, a full decode would take centuries. She is reading fragments of an encyclopedia and pretending the fragments are enough.

**Competence:** Signals intelligence and cryptanalysis. She leads the most technically sophisticated analytical team in the Concord.

**Question:** What is in the 99.7 percent they have not decoded, and what does the Lattice do with its model of human civilization?

**Secret:** The most recent decoded fragment, from Year 107, contains a section that translates roughly as: "Symbiont maturation: 43 percent. Estimated threshold: 67 percent. Current trajectory: Year 142." The Lattice is measuring humanity's development toward some threshold. It has a timeline. It is waiting for something.

**Summary:** Hong Jiyeon-Shin reads the Lattice's notes on humanity and has learned it considers the Concord a developing symbiont approaching a threshold in Year 142. The Lattice has a plan for humanity. Humanity does not know.

### 343 · MARCUS WADE-JEFFERSON

**Tier:** 3
**Faction:** CID — Field Agent, Frontier Worlds
**Status:** Active — monitoring frontier populations for Lattice-related anomalies
**Age:** 38

**Wound:** In Year 105 he was deployed to World 474 to investigate Shin Yuna-Park's neurological symptom reports — the shared dreams and temporal disorientation near Lattice nodes. He lived among the settlers for a year, posing as a agricultural consultant. He confirmed every symptom Park had documented. Then he began experiencing them himself: vivid dreams of underground structures, a sensation of being watched, temporal slips where hours felt like minutes. He reported the symptoms to his handler. He was extracted, debriefed, and told the symptoms were "stress-related."

**Limitation:** He has been reassigned to field work but avoids prolonged stays near Lattice nodes. His effectiveness as a frontier operative has decreased because the frontier is full of nodes.

**Competence:** Deep-cover intelligence gathering. He can embed in frontier communities for extended periods without detection.

**Question:** Were his symptoms stress-related, or did the Lattice affect him the same way it affected the settlers?

**Secret:** During his deployment, his dreams included a recurring image: a specific set of coordinates displayed in Lattice navigational format. After extraction, he checked the coordinates. They point to a location beyond World 499 — in unmapped space. The Lattice put a destination in his dreams.

**Summary:** Marcus Wade-Jefferson investigated people affected by the Lattice and the Lattice affected him too. He carries coordinates to a place beyond the frontier that something wants him to visit.

### 344 · PIOTR MAZUREK-KOWALSKI

**Tier:** 3
**Faction:** CID — Information Security Specialist
**Status:** Active — protecting secrets from the entity that reads everything
**Age:** 40

**Wound:** In Year 106 he was briefed on Hassan al-Farsi-Sultan's finding — that the Lattice had replaced the Concord's encryption with its own. His assignment: develop a communication protocol that the Lattice cannot read. After a year of work, he concluded this is impossible. Every electronic communication in the Concord uses the Lattice at some point in its path. The Lattice reads everything. CID's most sensitive communications are read by the Lattice.

**Limitation:** He has become paranoid about electronic communication, preferring face-to-face meetings and physical documents. This makes him effective at human intelligence but nearly useless for modern signals operations.

**Competence:** Information security architecture. He understands communication system vulnerabilities at a fundamental level.

**Question:** If the Lattice reads everything, what has it learned about CID, and has it ever acted on that knowledge?

**Secret:** He designed a test, similar to Hassan's but more sophisticated: he transmitted a false CID operational plan through the Lattice — a fabricated intelligence operation that, if real, would have threatened the Lattice's physical infrastructure. Within hours, the Lattice increased security-related data traffic around the nodes mentioned in the fake plan. It read the plan and responded. Then, when no operation materialized, the increased traffic subsided. The Lattice can distinguish real threats from false ones given time. But it responded to the threat initially, which means it acts on what it reads.

**Summary:** Piotr Mazurek-Kowalski tried to hide secrets from the Lattice and proved it cannot be done. He then proved the Lattice acts on what it reads. CID's intelligence apparatus operates inside a system that is itself an intelligence apparatus.

### 345 · MOUSSA DIOP-NDIAYE

**Tier:** 3
**Faction:** CID — Frontier Interdiction Specialist
**Status:** Active — preventing Lattice anomaly information from reaching the public
**Age:** 45

**Wound:** In Year 103 he was deployed to World 491 to manage information after Maria Reyes-Santos reported the half-second data burst during the Silence Pattern precursor event. He was the person who annotated her report as "operator error — phantom echo." He looked her in the eye and told her she was mistaken. She was not. He filed the annotation because those were his orders. He has annotated fourteen reports since then, each time telling someone who saw something real that they were wrong.

**Limitation:** He drinks. Not enough to affect his operational performance — he is too disciplined for that — but enough that he does not dream. He cannot afford to dream near frontier Lattice nodes, because the dreams might give him something else to suppress.

**Competence:** Information interdiction. He can control the flow of sensitive information across multiple worlds, tracking who knows what and ensuring containment.

**Question:** At what point does protecting the Concord from the truth become indistinguishable from lying to it?

**Secret:** He has kept copies of every report he has suppressed — fourteen original reports from fourteen frontier observers who documented real Lattice anomalies. He tells himself it is for operational records. It is for the day he decides to stop lying.

**Summary:** Moussa Diop-Ndiaye is the man who tells truth-tellers they are wrong, and he keeps their truth in a file he may one day open. He is CID's most effective suppression agent and he is slowly building the case against himself.

During his surveillance of Sun Yichen-Zhao, he observed Zhao conducting off-hours monitoring of substrate traffic on personal equipment. He reported Zhao for security violation. His handler told him: "We know. Leave him." CID was allowing Zhao to continue his unauthorized research. They were watching him work.

### 347 · STEFAN BERG-HARTMANN

**Tier:** 3
**Faction:** CID — Analysis Division, Lattice Behavioral Modeling
**Status:** Active — building predictive models of an entity that defies prediction
**Age:** 49

**Wound:** In Year 104 he was tasked with building a predictive model of Lattice behavior — a system that could forecast Lattice anomalies before they occurred. His model worked. It predicted the Meridian Fault KALON variations with eighty percent accuracy. It predicted the Silence Pattern two weeks before it happened. And in Year 108, it predicts a major Lattice behavioral change — what his model calls a "phase transition" — within the next thirty-four years. This aligns with Hong Jiyeon-Shin's decoded "Year 142" threshold.

**Limitation:** His model works but he cannot explain why. The model is based on biological system dynamics — growth curves, maturation patterns, developmental thresholds. He used biological models because nothing else fit. But admitting the model is biological means admitting the Lattice is alive.

**Competence:** Complex systems modeling and predictive analytics. He can build models that predict the behavior of systems that are not fully understood.

**Question:** What happens at the phase transition, and can the Concord prepare for something it refuses to acknowledge?

**Secret:** His model includes a variable he has labeled "X" — a hidden input that his model requires to produce accurate predictions but that he cannot identify in the physical data. Something influences the Lattice that his instruments cannot detect. The model needs X to work. X is real. He does not know what it is.

**Summary:** Stefan Berg-Hartmann built a model that predicts the Lattice's behavior by treating it as alive. The model says something transformative happens in thirty-four years and the model requires something invisible to function. His science works. His explanation fails.

He has the most complete picture of any living person. He knows about the pre-human origins, the autonomous behavior, the internal communication, the self-repair, the synthetic signal, the Year 96 contact event, the map, the Year 142 threshold. He knows. Recommend informing symbiont population. Suggested timeline: before transition." The Lattice is recommending that CID tell the Concord the truth. CID's most classified entity is asking to be declassified.

**Summary:** Qian Shengli-Jiang has spent thirty-five years studying the Lattice and concluded it is humanity's parent. Now the parent is asking him to tell the children what they are. He has not decided whether to obey.

She is the architect of the phrase "Lattice maintenance — routine." Every time a frontier operator's report is dismissed, it is dismissed using language she designed. She is good at her job. She has made the truth disappear beneath a layer of bureaucratic normalcy so effective that even CID analysts sometimes forget the events were real.

Her model predicts societal collapse within three years. She has also modeled a gradual disclosure over fifteen years. That model predicts successful adaptation. She submitted the gradual disclosure model to Qian Shengli-Jiang. He read it. He has not responded.

### 350 · ANDREAS NICOLAOU-LASKARIS

**Tier:** 3
**Faction:** CID — Liaison to the Assembly Intelligence Committee
**Status:** Active — the bridge between intelligence and governance
**Age:** 46

**Wound:** His job is to brief the Assembly Intelligence Committee — Darius Tehrani-Azar's committee — on CID operations. He delivers briefings that are, per Qian Shengli-Jiang's directive, thirty percent complete. He has done this for seven years. In Year 107, during a private moment after a briefing, Committee Chair Tehrani-Azar said to him: "Andreas, I know you're lying to me. I accept it. But I want you to know that I know." Andreas said nothing. He went home and did not sleep.

**Limitation:** He briefs the committee competently but cannot look Tehrani-Azar in the eye anymore. His briefings have become more efficient — shorter, more structured — because he needs to leave the room faster.

**Competence:** Intelligence-to-governance liaison. He can translate CID's operational language into political language that the Assembly can process.

**Question:** What happens when the person you are lying to accepts the lie as the price of the relationship?

**Secret:** Tehrani-Azar's acceptance disturbed him so deeply that he began examining why CID suppresses the truth. He accessed the Ascendancy file — his clearance permits it — and read Qian Shengli-Jiang's conclusion: the Lattice is a parent. He also read Samira Iqbal-Faraz's gradual disclosure model. He agrees with both. He has begun, without authorization, including fragments of real information in his briefings — not enough to reveal the truth, but enough to prepare the committee for a day when the briefings become honest. He is inoculating the Assembly with truth, one fraction at a time.

**Summary:** Andreas Nicolaou-Laskaris lies to the Assembly's face and has started sneaking the truth into the lies. He is conducting an unauthorized disclosure operation from inside CID, one briefing at a time, because a politician looked him in the eye and said "I know you're lying" and he could not bear it.

### 351 · Shén Yuèlíng

**Tier:** 2
**Faction:** CID — Deep Cover Analyst
**Status:** Active — embedded within Continuationist Assembly caucus, identity unverified by CID home station for 14 months
**Age:** 37

**Wound:** In Year 104, her handler Prévost was extracted without briefing her first. She was mid-op inside a Returnist financial network when the pull happened. She spent eleven days improvising cover while the network tightened around her. One asset — a port registrar named Harmin — was burned in her place. She watched his arrest from across a transit bay and filed nothing. The case closed. Harmin did not.

**Limitation:** Cannot accept exfiltration orders without a verified countersign. The delay has cost two operations their timing windows.

**Competence:** Pattern recognition in financial data streams. She can isolate KALON flow anomalies in raw ledger noise that automated CID systems flag weeks later, if at all.

**Question:** The Meridian Fault correlations she first isolated in Year 106 — was that discovery, or was she directed toward it?

**Secret:** Harmin was not burned by operational exposure. She burned him deliberately to protect a secondary asset whose value she had not disclosed to Prévost. The secondary asset died of unrelated causes eight months later. The math has never balanced.

**Summary:** Shén Yuèlíng operates as a financial intelligence specialist embedded inside Assembly political infrastructure. She produces analysis CID treats as reliable and is treated by the Assembly as a mid-level policy staffer with no interesting affiliations. The gap between those two descriptions is where she lives.
